California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 29, 1988

Donna Mayfield
4840 E. 58th Street, Apt. A
Maywood, CA 90270

Re: Your Requests for Advice
Our File Nos. I-88-034 and I-88-038

Dear Ms. Mayfield:

We have received your letter regarding the Maywood City
Council's adoption of an item which was not on the posted
agenda and your subsequent letter concerning the council's
consideration of a negative declaration on a redevelopment
project in your city.

The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to
interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act.1/ The
Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some
of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions,
disclosure of assets and income of public officials,
disqualification of public officials from decisions which could
affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the
activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on
public officials.

The questions you pose in your letters are not covered by
the Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on
these matters. It would be more appropriate for you to direct
your questions regarding the adoption of an item by the city
council to your county district attorney and the consideration
of the negative declaration to your city attorney.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Jeanette Turvill
Legal Assistant
Legal Division

Dear Citizens:

We have received your letter regarding the Maywood City

consideration of a negative declaration on a redevelopment

The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to
interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act.1/ The
Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some
of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions,
disclosure of assets and income of public officials,
disqualification of public officials from decisions which could
affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the
activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on

The questions you pose in your letters are not covered by

your questions regarding the adoption of an item by the city
council to your county district attorney and the consideration
of the negative declaration to your city attorney.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

FROM:

Louis Martinez
4654 E. 57th St. Apt. A
Maywood, California  90270
(213) 771-5368


Ronald E. Fick
6046 Vinevale Ave.
Maywood, California  90270


Donna Mayfield
4840 E. 58th St. Apt. A
Maywood, California  90270


Pat Quilas
6136 Pala Ave.
Maywood, California  90270
(213) 560-2849


Maywood Concerned Citizens
4626 E. Slauson Ave.
Maywood, California  90270
(213) 771-3841

January 11, 1988

Leonard Locher
Chief Administrative Officer
City Clerk
4319 E. Slauson Ave.
Maywood, California 90270

Dear Mr. Locher:

The Maywood City Council will hold a public hearing on January 12, 1988 at which
time the council will consider the adoption of a negative declaration (Exhibit"A")
and the vacation of Heliotrope Circle between Slauson and Mayflower. We, as voters
and concerned members of the community, hereby request that the council not adopt
this negative declaration and also to abandon their efforts to vacate this very
important street. The negative declaration is flawed and should not be substituted
for an environmental impact report.

This negative declaration is flawed because of the following reasons:
(1) It is based on an environmental checklist (Exhibit"B") which is defective
    because it is not supported by substantial evidence or facts.
(2) It is based on an environmental checklist which is not completely truthful,
    the vast majority of the traffic using Heliotrope Circle does so in order to
    continue south on the Circle and not because of the businesses on the Circle,
    as this checklist implies.
(3) It is based on an environmental checklist which at times contradicts itself
    as well as statements on this negative declaration.
(4) It is based on an environmental checklist which does not address any of the
    potentially adverse effects this project may have in the environment.
(5) It is based on an EIR, prepared in June of 1986, which does not mention or
    address the vacation of Heliotrope Circle.
(6) It is based on an EIR which does not include a study of the intersection of
    Slauson and Mayflower.
(7) It is based on an EIR which itself is defective because according to our survey
    the number of vehicles turning right (south) onto King and Heliotrope Avenues,
    of eastbound Slauson traffic, is incorrect. (Exhibit"D")
(8) It is based on a Traffic Committee recommendation of a meeting held on Nov.24,
    1987. At this committee meeting none of the members were given any evidence
    or facts on which to base their decision. (Exhibit"C"-minutes of that meeting)

We ask that the council not adopt this flawed negative declaration and also that they
follow any and all applicable Government Codes and regulations before proceeding any
further on this project. Public Resources Code 21168 requires that a local agency
prepare an environmental impact report if there is substantial evidence that a pro-
ject will have a significant environmental impact whether or not there is evidence
to the contrary. According to our survey (Exhibit"D"), between 150 and 175 vehicles
will need to be rerouted during peak hours because of this project, this fact alone
is sufficient to require an environmental impact report.

Locher
January 11, 1988
Page 2




Heliotrope Circle serves as a safe and quick exit from Slauson Avenue and thus helps
to relieve the congestion on this very heavily traveled street. Slauson, especially
during peak hours. This Circle also helps keep traffic off of our residential streets,
streets which are already crowded and are very narrow.

We ask that the council not make another arbitrary decision on this very important
issue and also that it not abuse it's discretion by adopting this negative declara-
tion on deciding to vacate Heliotrope Circle without the preparation of an environ-
mental impact report. If the council decides to violate Government Codes by deciding
against the best interest of the community, we, the members of-the community, will
have no other choice but to seek redress in the courts.




Very truly yours,

[signatures]


cc: Los Angeles County Grand Jury
cc: Los Angeles County District Attorney
cc: California State Attorney General
cc: Fair Political Practices Commission
cc: U.S. Congressman Matthew Martinez
cc: State Senator Art Torres
cc: Assemblywoman Lucille Royball Allard

LAW OFFICES OF

NIELSEN, MERKSAMER, HODGSON, PARRINELLO & MUELLER
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

SACRAMENTO                                650 CALIFORNIA STREET, SUITE 2650

1030 FIFTEENTH STREET, SUITE 250        SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
                                          TELEPHONE (415) 989-6800              FILE NUMBER
TELEPHONE (916) 446-6752


                                    January 13, 1988


Mr. Kevin Braaten-Moen
Technical Assistance
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Kevin:

     This letter is to confirm telephone advice by you on
this date with reference to Government Code section 84305 and the
disclaimers required for mass-mailings.

     In distinguishing between subparagraphs (a) and (b) of
Government Code section 84305, you stated that subsection (b)
applies to a general purpose recipient committee, a sponsored
committee, or a controlled committee that is controlled by only
one candidate. Subsection (a) applies to a controlled committee
that is controlled by more than one candidate.

     In applying the above statute, I described the "ABC
Committee, sponsored by XYZ," whose purpose is to oppose a ballot
measure. I further described the contents of a mailing by that
committee that consists of a fundraising letter by President
Gerald Ford on his personal stationery. You advised that so long
as the full name of the sponsored committee is shown on the
outside of the envelope, it is not necessary that the name of the
sponsored committee be printed on President Gerald Ford's
letterhead (or any other insert that might be enclosed in the
envelope).

     While I did not request advice on this additional
subject on the telephone, we now ask you to confirm that an
envelope imprinted with "President Gerald Ford (and his address
and city)" in the upper-left hand corner of the front of the
envelope and "ABC Committee, sponsored by XYZ (and its address
and city)" on the reverse flap of the envelope, constitutes
compliance with subsection (b) of Government Code section 84305.

Mr. Kevin Braaten-Moen
January 13, 1988
Page 2


          Will you please confirm that the above advice is
correct by signing a copy of this letter and returning it to me
as soon as possible.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Diane Elan Wick


The above advice is acknowledged as correct:

Fair Political Practices Commission


By:_________________________________

Title:_______________________________

Exhibit "A"

CITY OF MAYWOOD
4319 E. Slauson Avenue
Maywood, California, 90270

NEGATIVE DECLARATION

Disposition and Development Agreement

Description of Project:

Street Vacation - Heliotrope Circle from Slauson to Mayflower
Avenue. Vacation of street to facilitate the development of a
55 unit Senior Citizen Housing Complex.

Location of Project:

Located at and near Southwest corner of Slauson Avenue and
Mayflower Avenue

Project Proponent:

Maywood Building and Planning Department

Findings of Environmental Effect:

Based on the findings in the Environmental Checklist, it would
appear there would be no significant effect on the environment,
and would appear logical to claim a Negative Declaration for this
project.

Further Findings:

Additional mitigation measures are not necessary to avoid
potentially significant effects.

This Negative Declaration was prepared pursuant to the California
Environment Quality Act of 1970 (CEQA), Public Resources Code No.
21100 et, seq., and follows the EIR Guidelines of Resources on
February 3, 1973 as amended.

[Signature]
Brinda L. Dovalos
Building and Planning Assistant

Post on or before December 10, 1987

December 17, 1987


MEMORANDUM

TO:     Honorable Mayor and Members of Maywood City Council
        Leonard R. Locher, Chief Administrative Officer/
                         City Clerk

FROM:   Ronald L. Lindsey, Director of Building & Planning
        Linda L. Dovalis, Building & Planning Assistant

SUBJECT: Street Vacation
         Heliotrope Circle from Slauson Avenue to Mayflower Ave.

PROPONENT: Maywood City Staff

PROPOSAL: The Maywood Redevelopment Agency has acquired or has
         in escrow all of Lot 1824 and the east half of Lot
         1825 of Tract No. 4185 as per map recorded in Book
         44, Page 84. The Agency is obligated by a Disposition
         and Development Agreement to vacate such street and
         convey the property to a developer.

         The City wishes to cooperate with the Agency in the
         completion of its obligations under the DDA by
         vacating Heliotrope Circle pursuant to the Street
         Vacation Act of 1941.

         The Traffic Committee unanimously recommended that the
         Council conduct a Public Hearing and approve the
         vacation of Heliotrope Circle.

FINDINGS:

1.       Approval of the street vacation will not be detri-
         mental to the public interest, safety, health or wel-
         fare and will not be detrimental or injurious to the
         property or improvements in the vicinity and zone in
         which the property is located.

         An Environmental Impact Report prepared in June 1986
         states even with full build-out of Redevelopment Area
         No. 2, the projected future operating conditions at
         the intersections of Slauson/King and Slauson/
         Heliotrope would operate at "acceptable levels of ser-
         vice" during late afternoon peak hours.

2.       Approval of the vacation will not be contrary to, or
         in conflict with, the goals and objectives of the
         General Plan.

         One of the objectives of the Land Use Element of the

MEMORANDUM
December 17, 1987
Vacation of Heliotrope Circle
Page 2

General Plan for Commercial Land Use is to provide
multi-unit housing opportunities for Senior Citizens
and low income families.

3. Existing public utility facilities will be adequate
to serve the area. Emergency access will not be
hindered.

The right-of-way of Slauson Avenue is 110 feet wide,
and the right-of-way for Mayflower Avenue is 50 feet,
which is equal to the right-of-way of King Avenue
and of Heliotrope Avenue.

CALIFORNIA ENVIRONMENTAL AIR QUALITY ACT:

Based on the information in the environmental check list, a
Negative Declaration was prepared and posted, and a Notice of
Determination will be filed with the County Clerk.

POSSIBLE COUNCIL ACTION:

1. Approve as proposed and adopt the findings; or

2. Approve subject to other than proposed; or

3. Deny

RECOMMENDATION:

The Maywood Traffic Committee and Staff recommend approval of the
vacation of Heliotrope Circle from Slauson Avenue to Mayflower
AVenue including the traffic island.

III. DISCUSSION OF ENVIRONMENTAL EVALUATION

Soils will be subjected to displacement and compaction as well as uncovering as a result of new construction and relocation of certain utilities lines.

The land use will be changed from a street to residential living use. The circle will be closed and traffic will be rerouted to make a right turn at Mayflower, off Slauson.

b. Presently traffic flows directly off Slauson through Heliotrope Circle. By closing this portion of Heliotrope Circle, traffic will make a right turn at Mayflower, off Slauson.

c. Concerns have been raised about water pressure for the site after relocation of the water lines. These problems can be worked out through engineering.

General Discussion

The street currently serves three (3) businesses, one of which is and has been closed for years due to fire. The other two businesses have been acquired by the Maywood Redevelopment Agency and will be closed within four (4) months. Much of the traffic using the Circle has been for these businesses and also for the ARCO service station on the east side of Mayflower Ave. Under the former operation of the ARCO station there were pumps on the Heliotrope Circle side as well as Slauson Avenue side. Due to limited access from Slauson, customers would enter off Heliotrope Circle. When ARCO was remodeled, this problem was corrected and now a majority of the traffic enters off Slauson.

IV.  DETERMINATION
    (To be completed by Lead Agency)

    On the basis of this initial evaluation:


    It is the determination of the Environmental Impact
    Review Committee that the proposed project COULD NOT
    have a significant effect on the environment, and a
    NEGATIVE DECLARATION will be prepared.

    It is the determination of the Environmental Impact
    Review Committee that although the proposed project
    could have a significant effect on the environment,
    there will not be a significant effect in this case
    because the mitigation measures described on an at-
    tached sheet have been added to the project. A
    NEGATIVE DECLARATION will be prepared.

    It is the determination of the Environmental Impact
    Review Committee that the proposed project MAY have
    a significant effect on the environment, and an EN-
    VIRONMENTAL IMPACT REPORT is required.


    Other.

Leonard Locher 
Chief Administrative Officer 
City Clerk 
4319 E. Slauson Ave. 
Maywood, California 90270 
Dear Mr. Locher: 
January 7, 1988 
Govern~ent Code Section 54954.2 prohibits a local agency from taking action 
II on any item not appearing on the posted agenda ". At the regular meeting 
of the Maywood City Council on December 22, 1987, the council adopted res-
olution # 3838, an item which was not on the posted agenda. ( Enclosed is 
a copy of the posted agenda and a copy of the minutes of the meeting.) The 
approval of this resolution was a blatant violation of the Gov. Code Section 
known as the Brown Act. We do not understand why the council was allowed to 
violate this Gov. Code, especially with the presence of both the City Attorney 
and the Redevelopment Attorney. We demand that the Maywood City Council and 
the Maywood Redevelopment Agency refrain from any further violations of any 
applicable Gov. Codes or regulations when making public policy decisions.
sections.question_synthetic: Do questions regarding a city council's adoption of an agenda item not on the posted agenda and consideration of a negative declaration on a redevelopment project fall under FPPC jurisdiction under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC lacks jurisdiction to address these matters as they are not covered by the Political Reform Act; the requesters should instead direct their questions to the county district attorney and city attorney.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: Document consists of three nearly identical declination letters sent to different requesters on same date with same file numbers (I-88-034 and I-88-038). Also includes incoming request letter from Maywood citizens dated January 11, 1988, a lawyer's confirmation letter dated January 13, 1988 regarding Government Code section 84305 disclaimers, and a negative declaration document. The three FPPC responses all follow the same format declining jurisdiction and referring matters elsewhere. Confidence reduced slightly due to document being a compilation of multiple letters rather than single document, and some OCR quality issues with signatures.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do questions regarding a city council's adoption of an agenda item not on the posted agenda and consideration of a negative declaration on a redevelopment project fall under FPPC jurisdiction under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: The questions you pose in your letters are not covered by the Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on these matters. It would be more appropriate for you to direct your questions regarding the adoption of an item by the city council to your county district attorney and the consideration of the negative declaration to your city attorney.
embedding.first_500_words: / California Fair Political Practices Commission January 29, 1988 Donna Mayfield 4840 E. 58th Street, Apt. A Maywood, CA 90270 Re: Your Requests for Advice Our File Nos. I-88-034 and I-88-038 Dear Ms. Mayfield: We have received your letter regarding the Maywood City Council's adoption of an item which was not on the posted agenda and your subsequent letter concerning the council's consideration of a negative declaration on a redevelopment project in your city. The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act.1/ The Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions, disclosure of assets and income of public officials, disqualification of public officials from decisions which could affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on public officials. The questions you pose in your letters are not covered by the Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on these matters. It would be more appropriate for you to direct your questions regarding the adoption of an item by the city council to your county district attorney and the consideration of the negative declaration to your city attorney. Sincerely, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel [signature] By: Jeanette Turvill Legal Assistant Legal Division JET:jaj _________________________ 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 California Fair Political Practices Commission January 29, 1988 Pat Quiles 6136 Pala Avenue Maywood, CA 90270 Re: Your Requests for Advice Our File Nos. I-88-034 and I-88-038 Dear Ms. Quiles: We have received your letter regarding the Maywood City Council's adoption of an item which was not on the posted agenda and your subsequent letter concerning the council's consideration of a negative declaration on a redevelopment project in your city. The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act.1/ The Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions, disclosure of assets and income of public officials, disqualification of public officials from decisions which could affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on public officials. The questions you pose in your letters are not covered by the Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on these matters. It would be more appropriate for you to direct your questions regarding the adoption of an item by the city council to your county district attorney and the consideration of the negative declaration to your city attorney. Sincerely, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel [signature] By: Jeanette Turvill Legal Assistant Legal Division JET:jaj _______________________________ 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 California Fair Political Practices
embedding.summary: This document contains three declination letters from the FPPC (dated January 29, 1988) to different requesters (Donna Mayfield, Pat Quiles, and Maywood Concerned Citizens) regarding questions about Maywood City Council's adoption of an agenda item and consideration of a negative declaration, along with supporting correspondence and a negative declaration document.
